event.summary: Claimed one ready iteration unit without expanding repository or path scope.

acceptance.proof: The public fixture binds official binary/version provenance and private raw-evidence hashes while exposing only independently observed protocol labels, role order, bounds, and typed outcomes.
Only the selected stream-handshake feature plus the exact runtime marker opens the effect; default and mismatched inputs remain inert.
One caller-selected loopback listener and connector exchange the admitted request/response headers under finite byte/time limits with exact identity matching and deterministic close.
Malformed, oversized, timeout, cancellation, identity-mismatch, and immediate port-rebind paths return typed bounded outcomes without persistent work.
No sample transport, clock runtime, endpoint selection, device behavior, background recovery, unsafe/FFI, or Manifold authority is added.

document.commit_policy: Commit the evidence, closed activation, runtime, tests, and public routing coherently before validation; keep lifecycle transitions and push preparation intentional.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route the first bounded TCP handshake runtime and retain transport and authority exclusions.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Run owner, runtime-loopback, public-boundary, lock, and workflow gates.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Describe the opt-in bounded handshake slice without sample or broad interoperability claims.
Document explicit activation, socket ownership, bounds, cancellation, and cleanup.
Separate black-box framing evidence from the Rust runtime claim.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Run focused evidence and public-boundary gates.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Bind official package and private raw-observation hashes without publishing raw artifacts.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Run focused provenance and public-boundary gates.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route proportional framing, runtime, malformed-peer, cancellation, and cleanup checks.
Review direct-networking and public/private routing; no shared mutation expected.
Review closed activation, cleanup, and authority separation; no shared mutation expected.
Use the graph snapshot to reconcile source, activation, validation, and evidence surfaces; no shared mutation expected.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Retain the private deterministic graph snapshot.

non_scope: Samples, chunks, timestamps, clock exchange/correction, buffering, queues, retry, recovery, background threads, async runtimes, persistent sockets, multicast, interface enumeration, DNS, or ambient endpoint selection.
General HTTP parsing, arbitrary header extension policy, TLS, authentication, authorization, endpoint ownership/currentness, performance, non-loopback reachability, or ecosystem-wide compatibility.

Official implementation source, copied or translated source, dependencies, unsafe, FFI, devices, private artifacts, inbound-sample commands, or Manifold admission, registry, session, route, topology, lease, authorization, replay, or audit authority.
document.objective: Consume separately pinned public-safe black-box framing evidence as one dependency-free, explicitly activated, caller-configured TCP vertical slice: a bounded inlet request and outlet response exchange one accepted stream identity, terminate under finite connect/accept/read/write limits and cancellation, and release all sockets deterministically.
outputs: Sanitized request/response framing evidence with private raw bytes and endpoint/runtime values excluded.
One closed stream-handshake feature descriptor and resolved project lock.
Bounded synchronous inlet/client and outlet/server handshake APIs with typed errors, cancellation, malformed-peer coverage, and loopback cleanup evidence.
